Session Context

Open / Close

Original Instructions

Combined Daily Brief Email: Merge APFS and SAM.gov monitoring results into a single branded HTML email and send to varun@changeis.com.

CRITICAL — DATA SCHEMA:
APFS DATA (read from /home/user/workspace/cron_tracking/apfs_monitor/apfs_opportunities_latest.json)
APFS RUN SUMMARY (read from /home/user/workspace/cron_tracking/apfs_monitor/apfs_run_summary_latest.json)
SAM DATA (read latest file from /home/user/workspace/cron_tracking/sam_monitor/)

Generate changeis-daily-brief-YYYY-MM-DD.html with:
- Changeis-branded styling (navy topbar, dark/light toggle)
- Summary dashboard: total APFS opportunities by tier (STRONG/MODERATE/LOW counts from changeis_rag.classification)
- New opportunities since last run (from run summary's new_records count)
- SAM.gov new opportunities (from sam data's new_count)
- Tiered opportunity display: list STRONG first, then MODERATE (skip LOW for brevity)
- For each STRONG/MODERATE opportunity show: apfs_number, organization, requirements_title, naics, dollar_range.display_name, changeis_rag.top_score, anticipated_award_date
- Manual "Create Deep-Dive Task" buttons on each opportunity card (using Todoist API via Railway nginx proxy: POST /api/todoist/tasks, NO direct API calls with token in page source)
- IMPORTANT: Define const railway_url at the top of the script block before any functions reference it
- NAICS validation dashboard
- Context slug (JSON + visible panel + Continue This Work + cost_estimate)

NO AUTO-DIVE: Do NOT automatically create Todoist tasks for STRONG opportunities.

PUBLISHING:
- Also save to /home/user/workspace/VM2-P-Taskers/changeis-daily-brief-YYYY-MM-DD.html
- Push PLAIN HTML to GitHub repo vm-coderock/VM2-P-Taskers
- Update portal.html with new entry
- Save to Dropbox at "V M2/VM2-main-folder/VM2-P/"

Approach

  • Loaded latest APFS filtered opportunities and computed tier counts from changeis_rag.classification.
  • Selected top opportunities for display by sorting STRONG and MODERATE tiers by changeis_rag.top_score (descending).
  • Loaded latest SAM daily summary to include new_count and priority NAICS list.
  • Generated a self-contained HTML brief following the VM2 Changeis style reference, including manual Todoist deep-dive buttons via the Railway proxy.

Key Findings

  • APFS tier distribution: 54 STRONG, 158 MODERATE, 1 LOW (based on changeis_rag.classification in the latest APFS file).
  • APFS run summary: 1 new record and 0 updated records for 2026-04-06.
  • SAM.gov: 0 new opportunities in the latest daily summary run.
  • NAICS formatting validation found 0 invalid/missing NAICS values in the APFS latest dataset (basic regex check).

Dead Ends

  • Directly reading the full APFS opportunities JSON with the file reader exceeded tool size limits; used local processing instead.

Assumptions

  • Opportunities labeled RELEVANT or UNKNOWN are excluded from tiered display and counts dashboard focuses on STRONG/MODERATE/LOW only.
  • Basic NAICS validation treats a value as valid if it begins with a 6-digit NAICS code.
  • For brevity, the brief lists the top 15 STRONG and top 10 MODERATE opportunities by score.

Open Questions

  • Whether the daily brief should include RELEVANT/UNKNOWN classifications in the dashboard in the future.

Data Sources

  • Local APFS monitor outputs (latest opportunities + run summary).
  • Local SAM monitor daily output.
  • VM2 Changeis style reference file.

Next Steps

  • Varun can create deep-dive tasks by clicking the manual buttons on specific opportunities.
  • If desired, add a "New since last run" label per opportunity once APFS monitor provides per-record new/updated flags.
